name: Azerbaijan Energy Sites
description: Comprehensive GeoJSON dataset of energy infrastructure in Azerbaijan including offshore/onshore oil & gas fields, refineries, pipeline terminals, thermal power plants, hydroelectric power stations, and renewable energy sites. Coordinates in WGS84.

metadata.source: Compiled from SOCAR, BP, IEA, Wikipedia, Global Energy Monitor, Ministry of Energy of Azerbaijan, and offshore industry reports.

properties.name: Azeri-Chirag-Gunashli (ACG)
properties.location: Caspian Sea, ~100 km east of Baku
properties.notes: Largest oil development project in Azerbaijan; accounts for ~60-65% of national oil output.

properties.name: Shah Deniz Gas Field
properties.location: South Caspian Sea, ~70 km southeast of Baku
properties.export_route: South Caucasus Pipeline (SCP) → TANAP → TAP to Europe
properties.notes: One of the world's largest gas fields; backbone of the Southern Gas Corridor supplying gas to Europe.

properties.name: Absheron Gas Field
properties.location: Caspian Sea, ~100 km southeast of Baku; 25 km NE of Shah Deniz
properties.notes: Early Production System (EPS) first gas achieved July 2023. Major gas reserve boosting Azerbaijan's export capacity.

properties.name: Umid Gas-Condensate Field
properties.location: Caspian Sea, ~75 km south of Baku
properties.notes: SOCAR-operated field; initial platform with 3 wells. Second platform planned to increase gas output to 3 bcm/yr.

properties.name: Karabakh Oil & Gas Field
properties.location: Caspian Sea, ~120-130 km east of Baku; 15 km NE of ACG
properties.notes: BP acquired 35% operating stake from SOCAR in June 2025. Field previously explored by CIPCO (LUKAgip/Pennzoil consortium) in the 1990s.

properties.name: Shafag-Asiman Gas Field
properties.location: South Caspian Sea, deeper water south of Shah Deniz
properties.notes: Ultra-deepwater gas field under evaluation phase as of 2025. Partnership between BP and SOCAR.

properties.name: Babek Gas Field
properties.location: Caspian Sea, adjacent to Umid field south of Baku
properties.notes: Part of the Umid-Babek gas complex; combined estimated reserves of 600 bcm. Under further appraisal.

properties.name: Neft Dashlari (Oil Rocks)
properties.location: Caspian Sea, ~110 km east of Baku
properties.notes: World's first offshore oil platform (1949). Still operational with an extensive network of trestle roads and platforms. Historic Soviet-era mega-project.

properties.name: Pirallahi Oil Field
properties.notes: One of Azerbaijan's oldest oil fields, discovered in 1901. Located on Pirallahi Island in the Caspian Sea.

properties.name: Balakhani-Sabunchu-Ramana Oil Fields
properties.notes: Site of the world's first industrially drilled oil well (1847). Legacy onshore field complex on the Absheron Peninsula.

properties.name: Bibi-Heybat Oil Field
properties.notes: Historic nearshore field; partially reclaimed from the Caspian Sea. Contains the ATA construction yard for offshore platforms.

properties.name: Ashrafi–Dan Ulduzu–Aypara (ADUA)
properties.location: Caspian Sea, northeast of ACG block
properties.notes: BP acquired 35% operator stake alongside the Karabakh field in June 2025. Early-stage development.

properties.name: Sangachal Terminal
properties.location: Sangachal, ~30 km south of Baku center
properties.notes: Central hub for Azerbaijani oil and gas exports. Receives production from ACG (oil) and Shah Deniz (gas/condensate). Starting point of BTC and SCP pipelines.

properties.name: Heydar Aliyev Baku Oil Refinery
properties.modernization_phase: Phase 1 completed 2024 (Euro-5 fuels); Phase 2 ongoing (bitumen plant); Phase 3 by 2026
properties.notes: Major SOCAR refinery undergoing large-scale modernization since 2022. Gasoline capacity to rise to 2.2 Mt/yr upon completion.

properties.name: Sumgait Thermal Power Plant
properties.notes: One of Azerbaijan's significant gas-fired power stations supplying the industrial city of Sumgait.

properties.name: Shimal Thermal Power Plant
properties.notes: Shimal-1 (400 MW steam-gas, commissioned 2002) and Shimal-2 (commissioned September 2019) are key plants serving greater Baku.

properties.name: Baku Thermal Power Plant
properties.notes: Urban thermal plant including two gas-turbine units (53.5 MW each) funded by Bayerische Landesbank.

properties.name: Azerbaijan Thermal Power Plant (Mingachevir)
properties.notes: Largest power plant in Azerbaijan and the South Caucasus. Co-located with the Mingachevir HPP; known as Azerbaijan TPP.

properties.name: Janub Thermal Power Plant (Shirvan TPP)
properties.notes: Covers over 30% of Azerbaijan's electricity needs. Replaced the old Ali-Bayramli gas power station. Plans to add Garadagh Solar Power Plant adjacent.

properties.name: Nakhchivan Thermal Power Plant
properties.notes: Provides electricity to the Nakhchivan exclave, which has a separate power system from mainland Azerbaijan.

properties.name: Sheki Thermal Power Plant
properties.notes: Regional gas-fired power plant serving northwestern Azerbaijan.

properties.name: Mingachevir Hydroelectric Power Plant
properties.notes: Largest hydroelectric power station in the South Caucasus. Built 1945-1954. Overhauled in 2000 with new 70 MW turbine sets.

properties.name: Shamkir Hydroelectric Power Plant
properties.notes: Second largest HPP in Azerbaijan, situated on the Kura River. Provides approximately 12% of national electricity together with Mingachevir HPP.

properties.name: Yenikend Hydroelectric Power Plant
properties.notes: Built with EBRD financing; part of the Kura River hydro cascade.

properties.name: Tartar Hydroelectric Power Plant
properties.notes: Mid-sized hydroelectric plant on the Kura system.

properties.name: Araz Hydroelectric Power Plant
properties.notes: Joint Azerbaijani-Iranian hydroelectric station on the Aras River border.

properties.name: Varvara Hydroelectric Power Plant
properties.notes: Part of the Kura River hydro cascade system.

properties.name: Yeni Yashma Wind Power Park
properties.notes: Largest wind power project in Azerbaijan as of 2023. Located in Khizi District known for strong Caspian winds.

properties.name: Nakhchivan Solar Power Plant
properties.notes: Largest operating solar farm in Azerbaijan as of 2023; located in the sunny Nakhchivan region.

properties.name: Garadagh Solar Power Plant
properties.location: Garadagh district, southern Baku area / near Shirvan
properties.notes: Planned solar facility adjacent to the Janub TPP. Part of Azerbaijan's renewable energy expansion program.

properties.name: Sumgait Petrochemical Complex
properties.location: Sumgait industrial zone, north of Baku
properties.notes: Major industrial complex historically important for petrochemicals in the Soviet era; now partially upgraded under SOCAR.

properties.name: Baku-Tbilisi-Ceyhan (BTC) Pipeline – Baku Origin
properties.notes: Main crude oil export pipeline from Azerbaijan. Carries ACG crude from Sangachal to Ceyhan terminal on the Turkish Mediterranean coast.

properties.name: South Caucasus Pipeline (SCP/SCPX) – Baku Origin
properties.pipeline_destination: Turkish-Georgian border; connects to TANAP and TAP to Europe
properties.notes: Key export gas pipeline from Shah Deniz. Expanded (SCPX) for Shah Deniz Stage 2 to supply Southern Gas Corridor.

properties.name: Baku-Novorossiysk Pipeline – Baku Origin
properties.notes: Northern oil export route to the Russian Black Sea port of Novorossiysk. Alternative to BTC.

properties.name: Baku-Supsa Pipeline – Baku Origin
properties.notes: Western early export route for Azerbaijani oil to the Georgian Black Sea port of Supsa.

properties.name: Khachmaz Gas Power Plant
properties.notes: Regional gas-fired plant serving northern Azerbaijan.

properties.name: Astara Gas Power Plant
properties.notes: Southern regional power plant near the Iran border.

properties.name: Sangachal Gas Compressor Station
properties.notes: Gas compression and processing facility integrated within the Sangachal Terminal for Shah Deniz gas export.

properties.name: Quba Gas Power Plant
properties.notes: Regional gas-fired thermal plant serving Quba district.

properties.name: Ganja Thermal Power Plant
properties.location: Ganja, second largest city of Azerbaijan
properties.notes: Thermal power plant serving Azerbaijan's second largest city and the wider Ganja-Dashkasan region.

properties.name: Hajiqabul Wind Farm
properties.notes: Large-scale onshore wind project planned as part of Azerbaijan's renewable energy expansion following COP29 commitments.

properties.name: Alat Wind & Solar Complex
properties.location: Alat district, south of Baku (future Free Economic Zone)
properties.notes: Hybrid wind and solar project planned for the Alat Free Economic Zone area. Part of Azerbaijan's post-COP29 renewable targets.

properties.name: Shirvan Onshore Oil Fields
properties.notes: Cluster of onshore legacy oil fields in the Shirvan lowlands operated by SOCAR.

properties.name: Nakhchivan Small Hydropower Plants
properties.location: Nakhchivan Autonomous Republic, Aras River tributaries
properties.notes: Collection of small-run-of-river hydroelectric plants in the mountainous Nakhchivan exclave.

properties.name: Agsu–Goychay Small Hydropower Plants
properties.location: Agsu and Goychay districts, central Azerbaijan
properties.notes: Small hydropower facilities on mountain tributaries of the Kura River. Includes Goychay SHPP (3.1 MW) and Ismayilli SHPPs.
